Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Request Input A draft prompt for a benchmark Task The user wants me to act on this draft prompt

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Most likely interpretation The user is asking me to answer the prompt provided within the quotes effectively treating me as the subject of the benchmark

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Alternative Interpretation They might want me to refine the prompt itself

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Decision I will provide the SQL solution as requested by the prompt content

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the SQL Task Table event_log Columns case_id activity timestamp Goal Filter out cases with more than 6 events Return events for cases with 6 or fewer events

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Logic Count events per case_id Filter case_ids where count 6 Select all columns for those case_ids

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Approach 1 Window Function Use COUNT OVER PARTITION BY case_id to get the count per case in each row then filter

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Approach 2 Subquery or CTE Calculate counts in a CTE filter case_ids then join back or use IN

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Approach 3 HAVING clause Cant use HAVING directly on the main select without grouping the whole table which loses individual event rows So CTE or Window Function is best

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Comparison Option 1 is often more concise Option 2 is very explicit about the logic Both are valid DuckDB SQL

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: The Window Function approach is usually preferred for return rows tasks

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Since this is a Draft Prompt for the Benchmark the user might be looking for feedback on the prompt quality itself or the solution

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: If I just output the SQL I might miss the opportunity to help them improve the benchmark prompt If I critique the prompt I might not be following the instruction implied by pasting the prompt

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Safe Bet Provide the SQL solution clearly as that is the core task defined in the text Then add a section offering feedback on the prompt itself

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction on Plan Actually usually when users paste a Draft Prompt they want to see if the model understands it I should prioritize the solution

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait one more check Is the user asking me to write the prompt No they provided the draft Are they asking me to execute it Yes likely

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Okay I will generate the SQL query

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Query Construction Table event_log Columns case_id activity timestamp Condition COUNT OVER PARTITION BY case_id less than or equal to 6

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The IN clause is very readable and standard DuckDB handles it well

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Lets provide the Window Function approach as its often considered more advanced or performant Actually for benchmarking clarity is key The IN subquery is extremely clear

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Best approach Answer the SQL task showing I can do it AND provide feedback on the prompt showing I understand the meta task of benchmarking

Name: Validation - PE
Text: My query does exactly that
